question: Explain the purpose and usage of `public`, `private`, and `protected` access specifiers using a simple example with a base class `MyClass` and a derived class `DerivedClass`.

test_cases.expected:
MyClass constructor called with: 10, 20, 30
MyClass constructor called with: 100, 200, 300
DerivedClass constructor called. Derived public member: 400
--- Correct Version: C++ Access Specifiers (public, private, protected) ---

--- Access from main (MyClass object `baseObject`) ---
  main can access MyClass::publicMember directly: 10
  main cannot directly access MyClass::protectedMember (compile-time error).
  main cannot directly access MyClass::privateMember (compile-time error).
  main can access MyClass::privateMember via public getter: 30
  MyClass Data (from within MyClass):
    Public Member: 10
    Protected Member: 20
    Private Member (via public getter): 30

--- Access from main (DerivedClass object `derivedObject`) ---
  main can access DerivedClass::publicMember (inherited from MyClass): 100
  main can access DerivedClass::derivedPublicMember: 400
  main cannot directly access DerivedClass::protectedMember (inherited, compile-time error).
  main cannot directly access DerivedClass::privateMember (inherited, compile-time error).
  MyClass Data (from within MyClass):
    Public Member: 100
    Protected Member: 200
    Private Member (via public getter): 300

--- Access from DerivedClass member function (`demonstrateDerivedAccess`) ---
  Can access MyClass::publicMember: 100
  Can access MyClass::protectedMember: 200
  Cannot directly access MyClass::privateMember (compile-time error).
  DerivedClass's own publicMember: 400

--- End of Demonstration ---
